added: Windows: dispatchSyncRead ported from Mac — sync-read promise/future queue for GET endpoints
changed: Windows: all 16 module-level globals declared static, matching Mac
Windows: APICommand gained isSync and syncPromise fields, matching Mac

Windows: /avatars/state, /avatar/debug, /avatar/native-debug, /patterns/count, /patterns/{index}, /patterns/{index}/bbox, /patterns/{index}/input, /patterns/{index}/line-lengths, /arrangement-list, /pattern-arrangements, /arrangement/debug now route through dispatchSyncRead instead of calling CLO APIs from the HTTP thread
Windows: has_avatar_state_readback now advertised as true
Windows: queue drain timer corrected from 500ms to 200ms, matching Mac
Windows: every CLO API call inside a sync-read handler wrapped in try/catch so a single failed call cannot block the HTTP thread for the full timeout

Windows: read-pattern-count, read-pattern-info, read-pattern-bbox, read-pattern-input, read-arrangement-list now report success based on whether the primary CLO call actually returned, instead of always reporting success

AvatarPropertyDebugState struct, BuildAvatarPropertyDebugJson, StringMapToJson, and StringVectorToJson moved into shared/CloNativePluginSupport.h, removing duplicate definitions from the Windows and Mac source files
fixed: Windows: GET /avatars/state no longer crashes the plugin server via unhandled SEH — reads are now marshaled through the main-thread command queue

Windows: outer command-processing catch block now also catches non-std::exception throws via catch(...), so a sync command's promise is still fulfilled with a failure payload instead of leaving the HTTP thread to time out
resolved: Windows GET read endpoints calling CLO API directly from the HTTP thread — now uses the same dispatchSyncRead pattern as Mac
known_issues: Native avatar measurement CSV import remains under investigation
Windows export-avatar-avt: ExportAVT crashes CLO main thread via SEH exception — stubbed out, has_avatar_avt_export advertised as false; use zprj extraction

catch(...) in ProcessCommandQueue does not catch Windows SEH (hardware) exceptions under /EHs — a CLO read API raising SEH still blocks the sync promise for the full 3s timeout; full fix requires __try/__except wrappers (deferred to Phase 3)

notes: Use the latest allowed plugin only. Windows and Mac should match endpoint behavior before both are marked in_sync.
